title: phoradendron serotinum var. macrotomum
intro: Phoradendron serotinum var. macrotomum, also known as the deciduous mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species native to eastern North America, which grows on the branches of deciduous trees such as oak and maple. The medicinal properties of Phoradendron serotinum var. macrotomum include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality due to its sedative properties. Phoradendron serotinum var. macrotomum can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its aerial parts can be used to make a salve or ointment for topical application.
